heading: Corporate shareholder’s basis in stock reduced by nontaxed portion of extraordinary dividends
label: 1059 Corporate shareholder’s basis in stock reduced by nontaxed portion of extraordinary dividends
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1059 was renumbered .section 1063 of this title

Amendments2018—Subsec. (d)(3). substituted “and there shall not be taken into account any day which is more than 2 years after the date on which such share becomes ex-dividend.” for “; except that ‘2 years’ shall be substituted for the number of days specified in subparagraph (B) of section 246(c)(3).”Pub. L. 115–1412017—Subsec. (b)(2)(B). substituted “245, or 245A” for “or 245”.Pub. L. 115–972014—Subsec. (b)(2)(B). struck out “, 244,” after “243”.Pub. L. 113–2951998—Subsec. (g)(1). substituted “, in the case of stock held by pass-thru entities, and in the case of consolidated groups” for “and in the case of stock held by pass-thru entities”.Pub. L. 105–2061997—Subsec. (a)(2). , amended heading and text of par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In addition to any gain recognized under this chapter, there shall be treated as gain from the sale or exchange of any stock for the taxable year in which the sale or disposition of such stock occurs an amount equal to the aggregate nontaxed portions of any extraordinary dividends with respect to such stock which did not reduce the basis of such stock by reason of the limitation on reducing basis below zero.”Pub. L. 105–34, § 1011(a)Subsec. (d)(1). , amended heading and text of par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 105–34, § 1011(c)“(A) .—Except as provided in subparagraph (B), any reduction in basis under subsection (a)(1) shall occur immediately before any sale or disposition of the stock.In general“(B) .—In determining a taxpayer’s adjusted basis for purposes of subsection (c)(1), any reduction in basis under subsection (a)(1) by reason of a prior distribution which was an extraordinary dividend shall be treated as occurring at the beginning of the ex-dividend date for such distribution.”Special rule for computing extraordinary dividendSubsec. (d)(3). , substituted “subsection (a)” for “subsection (a)(2)”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1604(d)(1)Subsec. (e)(1). , amended heading and text of par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Except as otherwise provided in regulations, in the case of any redemption of stock which is—Pub. L. 105–34, § 1011(b)“(A) part of a partial liquidation (within the meaning of section 302(e)) of the redeeming corporation, or“(B) not pro rata as to all shareholders,any amount treated as a dividend under section 301 with respect to such redemption shall be treated as an extraordinary dividend to which paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a) apply without regard to the period the taxpayer held such stock.”Subsec. (e)(1)(A)(iii). , amended cl. (iii) generally. Prior to amendment, cl. (iii) read as follows: “which would not have been treated (in whole or in part) as a dividend if any options had not been taken into account under section 318(a)(4).”Pub. L. 105–34, § 1013(b)1989—Subsecs. (f), (g). added subsecs. (f) and (g) and struck out former subsec. (f) which read as follows: “.—The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section, including regulations providing for the application of this section in the case of stock dividends, stock splits, reorganizations, and other similar transactions and in the case of stock held by pass-thru entities.”Pub. L. 101–239Regulations1988—Subsec. (d)(5). , inserted “amount or” after “agrees to the”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(2), redesignated par. (6) as (5) and struck out former par. (5) which related to extension to certain property distributions.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(1)Subsec. (d)(6). , amended par. (6) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (6) read as follows: “Subsection (a) shall not apply to any extraordinary dividend with respect to any share of stock of a corporation if—Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(3)“(A) such stock was held by the taxpayer during the entire period such corporation (and any precedessor [sic] corporation) was in existence,“(B) except as provided in regulations, the only earnings and profits of such corporation were earnings and profits accumulated by such corporation (or any predecessor corporation) during such period, and“(C) the application of this paragraph to such dividend is not inconsistent with the purposes of this section.”, redesignated par. (7) as (6). Former par. (6) redesignated (5).Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(1)Subsec. (d)(7). , redesignated par. (7) as (6).Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(1)Subsec. (e)(1). , substituted “to which paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a) apply without regard to the period the taxpayer held such stock” for “for purposes of this section (without regard to the holding period of the stock)”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(4)Subsec. (e)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “Except as provided in regulations, the term ‘extraordinary dividend’ shall not include any qualifying dividend (within the meaning of section 243(b)(1)).”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(5)Subsec. (e)(3)(A). , amended subpar. (A) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (A) read as follows: “A qualified preferred dividend shall be treated as an extraordinary dividend—Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(6)“(i) only if the actual rate of return of the taxpayer on the stock with respect to which such dividend was paid exceeds 15 percent, or“(ii) if clause (i) does not apply, and the taxpayer disposes of such stock before the taxpayer has held such stock for more than 5 years, only to the extent the actual rate of return exceeds the stated rate of return.”Subsec. (e)(3)(B). , which directed the amendment of subpar. (B) “by striking out ‘subparagraph (A)’ and the material preceding clause (i) and inserting in lieu thereof ‘this paragraph’ ”, was executed by striking out “subparagraph (A)” in the material preceding clause (i) and inserting in lieu thereof “this paragraph”, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(8)(A)Subsec. (e)(3)(B)(ii). , substituted “clause (i)(II)” for “subparagraph (B)(i)(II)”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(8)(B)Subsec. (e)(3)(C)(i). , inserted “fixed” before “dividend payable” in introductory provisions and inserted at end “Such term shall not include any dividend payable with respect to any share of stock if the actual rate of return on such stock exceeds 15 percent.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(7)Subsec. (f). , inserted “and in the case of stock held by pass-thru entities” after “other similar transactions”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1006(c)(9)1986—Subsec. (a). , amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) read as follows: “If any corporation—Pub. L. 99–514, § 614(a)(1)“(1) receives an extraordinary dividend with respect to any share of stock, and“(2) sells or otherwise disposes of such stock before such stock has been held for more than 1 year,the basis of such corporation in such stock shall be reduced by the nontaxed portion of such dividend. If the nontaxed portion of such dividend exceeds such basis, such excess shall be treated as gain from the sale or exchange of such stock.”Subsec. (c)(1). , struck out “(determined without regard to this section)” after “such share of stock”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 614(c)(2)Subsec. (c)(4). , added par. (4).Pub. L. 99–514, § 614(b)Subsec. (d)(1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (1) read as follows: “Any reduction in basis under subsection (a) by reason of any distribution which is an extraordinary dividend shall occur at the beginning of the ex-dividend date for such distribution.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 614(c)(1)Subsec. (d)(3). , substituted “2 years” for “1 year”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 614(a)(3)Subsec. (d)(6). , added par. (6).Pub. L. 99–514, § 614(a)(2)Subsec. (d)(7). , added par. (7).Pub. L. 99–514, § 614(d)Subsecs. (e), (f). , added subsec. (e) and redesignated former subsec. (e) as (f).Pub. L. 99–514, § 614(e)

children.heading: Reduction in basis
children.label: 1 Reduction in basis
children.text: The basis of such corporation in such stock shall be reduced (but not below zero) by the nontaxed portion of such dividends.

children.heading: Amounts in excess of basis
children.label: 2 Amounts in excess of basis
children.text: If the nontaxed portion of such dividends exceeds such basis, such excess shall be treated as gain from the sale or exchange of such stock for the taxable year in which the extraordinary dividend is received.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of such dividend, over

children.label: B
children.text: the taxable portion of such dividend.

children.label: A
children.text: the portion of such dividend includible in gross income, reduced by

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of any deduction allowable with respect to such dividend under section 243 245, or 245A.1So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “extraordinary dividend” means any dividend with respect to a share of stock if the amount of such dividend equals or exceeds the threshold percentage of the taxpayer’s adjusted basis in such share of stock.

children.label: A
children.text: 5 percent in the case of stock which is preferred as to dividends, and

children.label: B
children.text: 10 percent in the case of any other stock.

children.label: i
children.text: which are received by the taxpayer (or a person described in subparagraph (C)) with respect to any share of stock, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which have ex-dividend dates within the same period of 85 consecutive days,

children.heading: Aggregation within 1 year where dividends exceed 20 percent of adjusted basis
children.label: B Aggregation within 1 year where dividends exceed 20 percent of adjusted basis
i
children.text: which are received by the taxpayer (or a person described in subparagraph (C)) with respect to any share of stock, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which have ex-dividend dates during the same period of 365 consecutive days,

children.label: i
children.text: the basis of such stock in the hands of such person is determined in whole or in part by reference to the basis of such stock in the hands of the taxpayer, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the basis of such stock in the hands of the taxpayer is determined in whole or in part by reference to the basis of such stock in the hands of such person.

children.heading: Fair market value determination
children.label: 4 Fair market value determination
children.text: If the taxpayer establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary the fair market value of any share of stock as of the day before the ex-dividend date, the taxpayer may elect to apply paragraphs (1) and (3) by substituting such value for the taxpayer’s adjusted basis.

children.heading: Time for reduction
children.label: 1 Time for reduction
children.text: Any reduction in basis under subsection (a)(1) shall be treated as occurring at the beginning of the ex-dividend date of the extraordinary dividend to which the reduction relates.

children.heading: Distributions in kind
children.label: 2 Distributions in kind
children.text: To the extent any dividend consists of property other than cash, the amount of such dividend shall be treated as the fair market value of such property (as of the date of the distribution) reduced as provided in section 301(b)(2).

children.heading: Determination of holding period
children.label: 3 Determination of holding period
children.text: For purposes of determining the holding period of stock under subsection (a), rules similar to the rules of paragraphs (3) and (4) of section 246(c) shall apply and there shall not be taken into account any day which is more than 2 years after the date on which such share becomes ex-dividend.

children.heading: Ex-dividend date
children.label: 4 Ex-dividend date
children.text: The term “ex-dividend date” means the date on which the share of stock becomes ex-dividend.

children.heading: Dividend announcement date
children.label: 5 Dividend announcement date
children.text: The term “dividend announcement date” means, with respect to any dividend, the date on which the corporation declares, announces, or agrees to the amount or payment of such dividend, whichever is the earliest.

children.heading: Exception where stock held during entire existence of corporation
children.label: 6 Exception where stock held during entire existence of corporation
i
children.text: such stock was held by the taxpayer during the entire period such corporation was in existence, and

children.label: ii
children.text: except as provided in regulations, no earnings and profits of such corporation were attributable to transfers of property from (or earnings and profits of) a corporation which is not a qualified corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to which the taxpayer holds directly or indirectly during the entire period of such corporation’s existence at least the same ownership interest as the taxpayer holds in the corporation distributing the extraordinary dividend, and

children.label: I
children.text: which were earned by, or

children.label: II
children.text: which are attributable to gain on property which accrued during a period the corporation holding the property was,

children.heading: Application of paragraph
children.label: C Application of paragraph
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any extraordinary dividend to the extent such application is inconsistent with the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Special rules for certain distributions
children.label: e Special rules for certain distributions

children.heading: Treatment of partial liquidations and certain redemptions
children.label: 1 Treatment of partial liquidations and certain redemptions
i
children.text: which is part of a partial liquidation (within the meaning of section 302(e)) of the redeeming corporation,

children.label: ii
children.text: which is not pro rata as to all shareholders, or

children.label: I
children.text: any options had not been taken into account under section 318(a)(4), or

children.label: II
children.text: section 304(a) had not applied,

children.heading: Reorganizations, etc.
children.label: B Reorganizations, etc.
children.text: An exchange described in section 356 which is treated as a dividend shall be treated as a redemption of stock for purposes of applying subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, the term “extraordinary dividend” does not include any qualifying dividend (within the meaning of section 243).

children.label: i
children.text: were earned by a corporation during a period it was not a member of the affiliated group, or

children.label: ii
children.text: are attributable to gain on property which accrued during a period the corporation holding the property was not a member of the affiliated group.

children.label: i
children.text: this section shall not apply to such dividends if the taxpayer holds such stock for more than 5 years, and

children.label: I
children.text: the qualified preferred dividends paid with respect to such stock during the period the taxpayer held such stock, over

children.label: II
children.text: the qualified preferred dividends which would have been paid during such period on the basis of the stated rate of return.

children.label: I
children.text: by only taking into account dividends during such period, and

children.label: II
children.text: by using the lesser of the adjusted basis of the taxpayer in such stock or the liquidation preference of such stock.

children.heading: Stated rate of return
children.label: ii Stated rate of return
children.text: The stated rate of return shall be the annual rate of the qualified preferred dividend payable with respect to any share of stock (expressed as a percentage of the amount described in clause (i)(II)).

children.label: I
children.text: provides for fixed preferred dividends payable not less frequently than annually, and

children.label: II
children.text: is not in arrears as to dividends at the time the taxpayer acquires the stock.

children.heading: Holding period
children.label: ii Holding period
children.text: In determining the holding period for purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii), subsection (d)(3) shall be applied by substituting “5 years” for “2 years”.

children.heading: Treatment of dividends on certain preferred stock
children.label: f Treatment of dividends on certain preferred stock

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any dividend with respect to disqualified preferred stock shall be treated as an extraordinary dividend to which paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a) apply without regard to the period the taxpayer held the stock.

children.label: A
children.text: when issued, such stock has a dividend rate which declines (or can reasonably be expected to decline) in the future,

children.label: B
children.text: the issue price of such stock exceeds its liquidation rights or its stated redemption price, or

children.label: i
children.text: to avoid the other provisions of this section, and

children.label: ii
children.text: to enable corporate shareholders to reduce tax through a combination of dividend received deductions and loss on the disposition of the stock.

providing for the application of this section in the case of stock dividends, stock splits, reorganizations, and other similar transactions, in the case of stock held by pass-thru entities, and in the case of consolidated groups, and
providing that the rules of subsection (f) shall apply in the case of stock which is not preferred as to dividends in cases where stock is structured to avoid the purposes of this section.
